key_points: Secularism refers to the separation of religion from the activities of the state
In a secular country, the government does not support or promote any particular religion
The Indian Constitution guarantees freedom of religion to all citizens
The state treats all religions equally and protects religious diversity
The government can intervene in religious practices that violate equality or human rights
Secularism helps maintain unity and peaceful coexistence in a diverse society
examples: Citizens are free to practice, profess and propagate any religion
The government can pass laws to stop harmful religious practices
Schools and public institutions do not promote any single religion
Courts can protect individuals if their religious freedom is violated
formulas: Secularism → Separation of religion and state
Religious freedom → Practice + Profession + Propagation of religion
Equality in religion → State neutrality + Equal protection of all religions
exam_tips: Explain secularism with reference to equality among religions
Mention the role of the Constitution in protecting religious freedom
Give examples showing how the state remains neutral
Explain how secularism promotes unity in a diverse country like India
